description: LTX-2.3 22B TWO-STAGE generation — the production-quality path Lightricks recommends. A first sampler pass renders the clip at base resolution, then the LTX-2.3 spatial upscaler (x2) upsamples the LATENT and a second sampler pass refines it, so you get sharper detail and steadier motion than the single-stage 'ltx2_generate' preset at the cost of a second pass. Text-to-video by default (image-to-video available, see _I2V). Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_T2V_I2V_Two_Stage_Distilled' (the cloud GemmaAPITextEncode nodes are bypassed; the local CLIPTextEncode + LTXAVTextEncoderLoader path is active, so no external API key is needed).

about.what: Creates a brand-new video from a written description (or a starting image), then runs it through a second high-detail pass that doubles the resolution and cleans up motion — the higher-quality sibling of the basic Generate preset.
about.inputs: A written description of the scene and action. Optionally a starting image for image-to-video (see notes).
about.key_knobs: Prompt — one flowing paragraph (scene, action, camera move, lighting). Resolution — the FIRST-pass size; the final clip is 2x bigger. Frames — clip length (must be 8*n+1).

_TEMPLATE: READY-TO-RUN. All four weights are pinned to real, ungated HF URLs (same dev checkpoint + gemma encoder as ltx2_generate, the official rank-384 distilled LoRA, and the LTX-2.3 spatial upscaler x2). Run --convert-only ONCE to validate the converted graph before a paid render.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: the public yanwk image runs comfy_run.py, which clones node_packs, downloads the weights, converts the UI graph to API via /object_info (the two ResizeImageMaskNode DynamicCombo nodes are handled by the converter), applies patches, optionally splices a Tier-1 --lora stack onto the checkpoint (anchor 3940 -> both passes), and renders. The second (upscale) pass makes this heavier than single-stage generation; budget extra time/VRAM. rtxpro6000 recommended.
_TWOSTAGE: Stage 1 samples at the EmptyLTXVLatentVideo size (node 3059, default 960x544). LatentUpscaleModelLoader (4974) loads the spatial upscaler x2 and LTXVLatentUpsampler (4975) doubles the latent; the second SamplerCustomAdvanced (4971) refines it. So the SAVED clip is ~2x the width/height you set here (default -> ~1920x1088). To trade quality for speed, mute the second pass / upsampler and save stage-1 directly (edit the converted workflow_api.json).
_I2V: Ships as TEXT-TO-VIDEO: the i2v init-image path is bypassed by default (PrimitiveBoolean 'bypass_i2v' node 4987 = true). For IMAGE-TO-VIDEO, provide a start frame and turn the bypass off: `--input start.png --set 2004.inputs.image=start.png --set 4987.inputs.value=false`. The init image conditions the first frame; everything else follows the prompt.
_DISTILL: Uses Lightricks' official rank-384 distilled LoRA (ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384-1.1) at the graph's tuned 0.5, matching the upstream example. If you stack reference-driven / i2v conditioning and detail looks dampened, swap to the cond-safe rank-72 variant the IC-LoRA presets use (TenStrip ...ceil72_condsafe) by editing the LoraLoaderModelOnly (4922) lora_name and this models entry. See README 'Cond-safe distill LoRA'.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Two-Stage — HQ generate (base pass + 2x latent upscale)

primary_input.label: Init image (optional, i2v)
primary_input.tooltip: Optional starting frame. Ships as text-to-video (this is ignored unless you turn the i2v bypass off — see the preset's _I2V note). Add catalog effect LoRAs with the LoRA picker.

prompt.default: A lone lighthouse keeper climbs a spiral staircase at dawn, lantern in hand, warm light spilling across weathered stone walls. The camera follows in a slow upward dolly, shallow depth of field, the distant sound of waves and a creaking door.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 works best as ONE flowing present-tense paragraph covering: shot type, scene + lighting, action over time, character details, camera move, and audio. LoRA trigger words are auto-appended. (src: LTX-2.3 prompt guide, ltx.io)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Lightricks' shipped LTX-2 negative (from their official example workflow).

ui.label: Width (stage-1; output is 2x)
ui.tooltip: FIRST-pass width; the spatial upscaler doubles it, so the saved clip is ~2x this. Keep stage-1 modest (the upscaler adds the resolution) -- 960 -> ~1920 is the sweet spot. Divisible by 32. (src: LTX-2.3 two-stage example workflow)

ui.label: Height (stage-1; output is 2x)
ui.tooltip: FIRST-pass height; doubled by the upscaler (544 -> ~1088). Divisible by 32. (src: LTX-2.3 two-stage example workflow)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 121, 161). 121 frames is ~4s at the ~30fps container. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)
